summary: End-to-end testing of the routing self-healer feature (services/routing_healer.py + admin endpoints + Heal routing UI button). 11/11 pytest backend tests passed; UI flow verified — Heal routing button sits next to Refill now, triggers POST /admin/routing-healer/run, returns 200 with {checked, healed, orphans} and shows the correct toast. Scheduler logs confirm 'Added job routing_healer_tick' on startup. No crashes even though Cloudflare is intentionally NOT configured in this preview env.
critical_code_review_comments: services/routing_healer.cleanup_orphan_pool_entries() lazy-imports get_cloudflare_config from routers.admin — same tight router→service coupling already flagged in iter15. Consider extracting cloudflare config helpers into services/cloudflare_config.py to avoid future circular-import risk.

_probe_traefik_route() uses httpx with verify=ssl.CERT_NONE to peek at the Traefik default cert. The peer-cert lookup r.extensions['network_stream'].get_extra_info('ssl_object') is undocumented httpx internals — could silently break on httpx upgrade. Add a unit test or fall back to TLS handshake via raw ssl.SSLContext to make this more robust.

routing_healer_tick caps at 25 apps/tick and 3 retries/hour — good defense. But routing_heal_attempts is $inc'd without an explicit reset path other than the 1h cooldown branch; if cooldown's last-heal-at parse fails we set attempts=0 in-memory only (not persisted). Consider an explicit $set: {routing_heal_attempts:0} when the cooldown elapses so the persisted counter actually resets.

heal_app() and routing_healer_tick() use slightly different field names when persisting: heal_app writes routing_last_healed_at while the tick writes routing_last_heal_at. Pick one (last_heal_at) and update both code paths so downstream queries / UI don't have to know both names.

POST /admin/apps/{id}/heal-routing returns {ok:false, error:'app not found'} with HTTP 200 — slightly unusual (most REST APIs would 404 here). Functionally fine since the UI parses .ok, but a 404 with the error in detail would be more REST-conventional.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (button placement, API call 200, toast displayed correctly)

seed_data_creation: test_heal_app_with_seeded_doc inserts a temporary db.apps doc with id=test-heal-<uuid8> and unconditionally deletes it in a finally block. No persistent test data remains after the run. subdomain_pool_target regression test saves and restores the original value.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cloudflare is intentionally NOT configured in this preview env. cleanup_orphan_pool_entries() correctly returns {checked:0, released:0, skipped:'cloudflare not configured'} and the healer tick returns {checked:0, healed:0, orphans:{...}} with no crash. The Heal routing UI button is at /app/admin → Platform Domain tab (note: route is /app/admin, NOT /dashboard/admin). data-testids verified: admin-routing-heal, admin-pool-refill, admin-subdomain-pool, pool-free-count, pool-health-label, admin-pool-target. Toast displayed: 'No live apps with managed FQDNs to check.' Scheduler log confirms 'apscheduler.scheduler - INFO - Added job "routing_healer_tick" to job store "default"' on each backend reload.
verified_behaviors: POST /api/admin/routing-healer/run: 401 anon, 403 demo, 200 admin → {checked:0, healed:0, orphans:{checked:0, released:0, skipped:'cloudflare not configured'}}
POST /api/admin/apps/{seeded_with_fqdn}/heal-routing: 200 with full {ok, fqdn, before, action, after} structure — no crash on unreachable host + fake coolify uuid
POST /api/admin/apps/{seeded_without_fqdn}/heal-routing: 200 with {ok:false, error:'app has no Cloudflare FQDN'}
RBAC: 401 anon, 403 demo on both endpoints
Scheduler: 'Added job routing_healer_tick' present in backend logs at every startup; job runs every 2 min as configured
Regression: GET /admin/subdomain-pool, POST /admin/subdomain-pool/refill, PUT /admin/platform-settings {subdomain_pool_target} all still work and round-trip correctly
Import sanity: services.routing_healer exports heal_app, routing_healer_tick, cleanup_orphan_pool_entries, _probe_traefik_route with no circular import
Frontend: 'Heal routing' (data-testid=admin-routing-heal) button visible at x=1005 y=1372, 'Refill now' (data-testid=admin-pool-refill) at x=1126 y=1372 — same row, side-by-side as required
Frontend integration: clicking Heal routing fires POST /admin/routing-healer/run (200) and shows sonner toast 'No live apps with managed FQDNs to check.' (matches the checked=0 branch in runHealer())
